title: FORA MergeBlockRuleSet
description: Versioned registry of merge-block rules (FORA-495 / Phase 3 §D of FORA-394). The set is the single source of truth for autonomous merge blocks. Every block is human-clearable through a typed escalation; no autonomous clears. Append-only within a major version; relaxing a block (or removing the no-autonomous-clears pin) is a major version bump plus a board approval.

contractId.description: Audit join key. Stable across the major version.

contractVersion.description: Semver. A major bump signals a breaking shape change OR any relaxation of a block (including dropping the no-autonomous-clears pin). Major bumps require a board approval via request_board_approval (ADR-0008).

issuedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this revision was published.

issuedBy.description: agent:<id> or user:<id>. Defaults to agent:devops.

supersedes.description: Optional UUID of the prior rule-set revision. Populated for minor and major bumps; null for the initial revision.

notes.description: Free-form human-readable rationale for this revision (e.g. 'add qa.tier.not_implemented rule').

rules.description: Append-only within a major version. Each rule carries the full MergeBlockRule shape.

Severity.description: The minimum severity of the upstream finding that triggers the rule.

Source.description: Where the predicate looks. convention covers BranchProtectionPolicy (FORA-245) and the synthetic no-autonomous-clears pin.

Escalation.description: Role-of-record for the human-clear path. 'human' = the requesting developer on the PR. 'board' triggers a request_board_approval interaction (ADR-0008).

AuditEventKind.description: The audit event type emitted by this rule.

Predicate.description: A minimal, declarative predicate over the upstream artifact. The first matching rule wins; later rules are shadowed.

kind.description: The predicate flavour. 'jsonpath' matches a JSONPath expression against the upstream artifact; the others are small literal comparators.

expression.description: The expression body. For 'jsonpath' this is the path (e.g. '$.verdict'); for 'equals' / 'gte' / 'lte' this is a field name; for 'and' / 'or' / 'not' this is a JSON array of nested Predicate objects.

value.description: The literal to compare against. Required for equals / contains / gte / lte. Omitted for jsonpath (truthy by presence), and, or, not, always.

MergeBlockRule.description: One merge-block rule. The full shape every rule in the default set carries.

ruleId.description: Stable dotted identifier. Append-only within a major version. Namespacing convention: <sourceFamily>.<findingShape>.<scope>.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the rule. One sentence.

blocks.description: If true, a matching upstream artifact blocks the merge autonomously. If false, the rule produces a warning only (audit event still emitted, but no gate). coverage.regression is the canonical warn-only rule.

escalation.description: One or more roles. A block requires every named role to clear. 'board' is exclusive (no other role can be combined).

slaHours.description: Hours the human has to clear after the block fires. 0 means immediate (no clearing path; the block is sticky). 168 = 1 week cap.

externalPolicyRef.description: Optional reference to an external policy that owns the predicate evaluation. convention.branch_protection points at FORA-245's BranchProtectionPolicy (mcp-github is the source of truth; no wrapper).

warnOnly.description: If true, the rule never blocks regardless of the blocks field. Reserved for coverage.regression and future warn-only rules.

metadata.description: Free-form structured annotation. Tooling may surface it on the PR; humans see description.

allOf.description: If blocks is true the rule must escalate to at least one human role. A non-escalating block is a misconfiguration.
warnOnly rules never block. blocks must be false.
If escalation includes 'board' it must be the only entry.
slaHours=0 is reserved for the secrets.detected rule (and any future sticky-block rule). blocks must be true.

PinnedRule.description: A synthetic rule whose only purpose is to pin a Phase-3 invariant. The convention.no_autonomous_clears rule is the canonical example: it pins 'no autonomous clears', so relaxing it requires a major version bump AND a board approval.
